title: symplocos quitensis subsp. boliviana
intro: Symplocos quitensis subsp. boliviana, also known as Bolivian snowberry, is a shrub or small tree species found in the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Bolivian snowberry include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used to make a topical cream or ointment for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
